data.title: British Airways owner IAG orders 53 Boeing, Airbus planes for long-haul fleet
data.description:
IAG, parent firm of British Airways and Spanish airline Iberia, announced orders for 53 new Boeing (NYSE:BA) and Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) planes for its long-haul fleet, a day after the U.S. reached a new trade deal with the U.K. 

IAG is ordering 32 Boeing (NYSE:BA) 787-10 aircraft for British Airways, and 21 Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) A330-900neo planes, which can be deployed within its carriers Aer Lingus, Iberia or LEVEL.

The new aircraft, which will enable IAG's airlines to grow and replace their long-haul fleets with modern, fuel-efficient planes, will be delivered between 2028 and 2033.

British Airways is also granted the rights to choose to buy up to 10 additional Boeing (NYSE:BA) 787 aircraft, while IAG can elect to purchase up to 13 additional Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) A330-900neo planes.

The new orders follow orders already placed in March for six Airbus A350-900s for Iberia, as well as six Airbus A350-1000s and six Boeing 777-9s for British Airways.

"Looking ahead to the next decade, these new aircraft will enable us to strengthen our core markets and further improve our customer experience, while continuing to drive long-term value for our shareholders," said IAG CEO Luis Gallego.

U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Thursday said a British airline was preparing to buy $10B worth of Boeing (BA) aircraft, sending the aircraft maker's shares up 3.3%.

data.title: Wintrust Financial prices $425M preferred stock offering
data.description:
Wintrust Financial (NASDAQ:WTFC) priced an underwritten public offering of 17M depositary shares with a liquidation preference of $25.00 per share for gross proceeds of $425M.

Each depositary share represents a 1/1,000th interest in a share of Wintrust’s 7.875% Fixed-Rate Reset Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series F.

The offering is expected to close on May 22, 2025.

Net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, which may include the redemption of all or a portion of its outstanding shares of Series D preferred stock and/or Series E preferred stock and the corresponding depositary shares representing interests in the Series E preferred stock, subject to approval from the Federal Reserve.

Wintrust intends to apply to list the depositary shares on Nasdaq under the symbol “WTFCN.”

data.title: Hims & Hers Health prices upsized $870M convertible senior notes
data.description:
Hims & Hers Health (NYSE:HIMS) priced its offering of $870M aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering to persons reasonably believed to be qualified institutional buyers.

The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $450M aggregate principal amount of notes.

The issuance and sale of the notes are scheduled to settle on May 13, 2025.

Initial purchasers of the notes have an option to purchase, for settlement within a period of 13 days from, and including, the date the notes are first issued, up to an additional $130M aggregate principal amount of notes.

Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including accelerating global expansion through both organic growth and strategic acquisitions. 

The stock price traded 1.4% lower on Thursday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: SolarBank renews base shelf prospectus
data.description:
• SolarBank (NASDAQ:SUUN) Thursday announced that it has filed a final short-form base shelf prospectus with the securities regulatory authorities in each of the provinces of Canada.
  • The Shelf Prospectus replaces the company's prior final short form base shelf prospectus dated May 2, 2023 which would have expired in June 2025.
  • A corresponding shelf registration statement on Form F-10 has been filed with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission but is not yet effective.
  • The Shelf Prospectus will enable the company to make offerings of up to C$200 million of common shares, debt securities, warrants, subscription receipts, units and share purchase contracts.

data.title: Kongsberg Gruppen ASA reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Kongsberg Gruppen ASA press release (OTCPK:NSKFF): Q1 Revenues of NOK 14 622M in revenues, 28% growth from Q1 2024, 19% adjusted for the sale of the steering gear and rudder business
  • NOK 2 892M EBIT in the quarter, of which NOK 1 048M was related to a gain from sale of the steering gear and rudder business
  • EBIT margin in the quarter was 19,8%, adjusted for gain from sale of the steering gear and rudder business 13,6%.
  • EPS of NOK 12,94.

data.title: Trump administration seeks tariff reductions and rare earths concessions in China talks - report
data.description:
The Trump administration is reportedly considering a significant reduction in tariffs during weekend talks with China, aiming to ease tensions and mitigate the economic strain both countries are beginning to experience.

Sources familiar with the preparations told Bloomberg that the U.S. has set an initial goal of cutting tariffs to below 60%, a move they believe China may be willing to reciprocate.

Also, high on the US wish list is securing the removal of China’s export restrictions on rare earths used to make magnets, as a range of industries face disruption, the people said.

China imposed new restrictions in April on the export of six heavy rare earth elements—metals that are refined exclusively in China, as well as rare earth magnets.

Beijing produces around 90% of the world's rare earths, a group of 17 elements used across the defense, electric vehicle, energy and electronics industries.

Progress has also been made on the fentanyl issue, according to the sources, who said separate discussions may soon take place to curb Chinese exports of the ingredients used to produce opioids.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday said that China "very much" wanted to make a trade deal, and that he "could be" open to lowering the tariff rate on the Asian nation if upcoming talks in Switzerland go well.

 

China ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (PGJ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (ASHR), (YIN), (YANG), (GXC), (FXP), (KBA), (CXSE), (CNYA), (CHAU), (FLCH), (YXI), (FCA), (KALL), (JCHI)

ETFs to track the S&P (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).
